UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account in the Nightcap Nosh application, storing profile information and preferences. It links to an external authentication system (Firebase).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firebaseUid.description: Unique identifier from Firebase Authentication for the associated user account. (Relationship: Firebase 1:1 UserProfile)

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication.

phoneNumber.description: The user's contact phone number. Mandatory for regular users for delivery coordination.

dateOfBirth.description: The user's date of birth, mandatory for age verification.

ageVerified.description: Flag indicating whether the user's age has been successfully verified to comply with liquor sales regulations.

deliveryAddressIds.description: References to saved delivery addresses for the user. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Address)

loyaltyPoints.description: Current loyalty points accumulated by the user for the rewards program.

isAdmin.description: Flag indicating if the user has administrative privileges for the Admin Dashboard.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product available in the catalog, including liquors and snacks. Includes details necessary for display and inventory management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

brand.description: The brand or distillery of the product.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

imageUrl.description: URL to a high-quality image of the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the category this product belongs to (e.g., 'Whiskey', 'Chips'). (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

isAgeRestricted.description: Flag indicating if the product requires age verification for purchase (e.g., liquor).

productType.description: The general type of the product (e.g., 'liquor', 'snack').

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of the product available in stock.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Organizes products into logical groups for easy browsing and discovery within the catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Spirits', 'Savory Snacks').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's purchase request, tracking its status from placement to delivery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'processing', 'out-for-delivery', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and any fees.

deliveryAddressId.description: Reference to the delivery address specified for this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

driverId.description: Reference to the driver assigned to deliver this order. (Relationship: Driver 1:N Order)

paymentMethod.description: The method of payment selected by the customer (e.g., 'cash_on_delivery', 'digital_wallet').

expectedDeliveryTime.description: The anticipated date and time for the order delivery.

actualDeliveryTime.description: The actual date and time when the order was delivered.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Details a specific product included within an order, capturing quantity and price at the time of purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the product being ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

priceAtOrder.description: The price of the product at the moment the order was placed.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores geographical address details, used for user profiles and order deliveries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who owns this address. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

street.description: The street name and house number.

state.description: The state or province of the address.

zipCode.description: The postal or zip code of the address.

isDefault.description: Flag indicating if this is the user's default delivery address.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a delivery driver responsible for fulfilling orders, including their current location for real-time tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Driver entity.

currentLatitude.description: The current latitude coordinate of the driver's location for real-time tracking.

currentLongitude.description: The current longitude coordinate of the driver's location for real-time tracking.

status.description: The current operational status of the driver (e.g., 'available', 'on_delivery', 'offline').

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a customer support chat conversation between a user and an agent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who initiated the chat session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatSession)

agentId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the support agent handling the session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatSession)

startTime.description: The date and time when the chat session started.

endTime.description: The date and time when the chat session ended or was closed.

status.description: The current status of the chat session (e.g., 'open', 'closed', 'resolved').

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents an individual message within a customer support chat session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the chat session this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N ChatMessage)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (user or agent) who sent this message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: The date and time the message was sent.

content.description: The text content of the chat message.

Reward.title: Reward
Reward.description: Defines a specific reward offered as part of the loyalty program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Reward entity.

name.description: The name of the reward (e.g., '10% Off Next Order', 'Free Snack').

description.description: A detailed description of the reward and its benefits.

pointsRequired.description: The number of loyalty points required to claim this reward.

value.description: The monetary value or percentage of the reward (e.g., 10 for 10% off, 5.99 for a free item's price).

validUntil.description: The date and time until which the reward is valid, if applicable.

UserReward.title: UserReward
UserReward.description: Tracks a specific reward earned or claimed by a user within the loyalty program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserReward entity.

userId.description: Reference to the user who earned this reward. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserReward)

rewardId.description: Reference to the specific reward definition. (Relationship: Reward 1:N UserReward)

earnedDate.description: The date and time when the user earned this reward.

redeemedDate.description: The date and time when the user redeemed this reward.

status.description: The current status of the user's reward (e.g., 'earned', 'redeemed', 'expired', 'available').

Pairing.title: Pairing
Pairing.description: Represents a curated pairing of a liquor product and a snack product, intended for easy discovery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pairing entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the pairing (e.g., 'Whiskey & Dark Chocolate').

description.description: A brief explanation of why these products pair well together.

liquorProductId.description: Reference to the liquor product in this pairing. (Relationship: Product 1:N Pairing)

snackProductId.description: Reference to the snack product in this pairing. (Relationship: Product 1:N Pairing)

imageUrl.description: URL to a high-quality image representing the pairing.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID is the user's Firebase UID (`userId`). Access is restricted to the owning user and administrators. Includes an `isAdmin` field for role-based access, but true admin access will be managed via the `/admin_roles` collection.

Stores delivery addresses belonging to a specific user. The document ID is `addressId`. Includes denormalized `userId` matching the parent for authorization independence. Accessible only by the owning user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier of the address document.

definition.description: Tracks loyalty rewards earned or claimed by a specific user. The document ID is `userRewardId`. Includes denormalized `userId` matching the parent for authorization independence. Accessible only by the owning user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who earned/claimed the reward.

params.name: userRewardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user reward document.

definition.description: Stores product categories. Globally readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

definition.description: Stores product details. Globally readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores curated product pairings. Globally readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: pairingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pairing.

definition.description: Stores definitions of loyalty program rewards. Globally readable by all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: rewardId
params.description: The unique identifier of the reward definition.

definition.description: Stores customer orders. Includes denormalized `userId` for customer access and `driverId` for assigned driver access, enabling authorization independence. The `status` field provides explicit state modeling. Accessible to the owning user, assigned driver, and administrators.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a specific order. Includes denormalized `userId` and `driverId` from the parent `Order` for authorization independence. Accessible to the owning user, assigned driver, and administrators, mirroring parent order access.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item.

definition.description: Stores driver profiles. The document ID is `driverId`. The `status` field provides explicit state modeling. Accessible to the owning driver and administrators.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique identifier of the driver.

definition.description: Stores customer support chat sessions. Includes denormalized `userId` for customer access and `agentId` for assigned agent access, enabling authorization independence. The `status` field provides explicit state modeling. Accessible to the owning user, assigned agent, and administrators.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat session.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a chat session. Includes denormalized `userId` and `agentId` from the parent `ChatSession` for authorization independence. Accessible to the owning user, assigned agent, and administrators, mirroring parent chat session access.

params.name: chatSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent chat session.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat message.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). Stores the UIDs of users who have administrative privileges. Used by security rules (e.g., `get(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`) to grant write access to administrative collections. Documents in this collection can be minimal, e.g., just `id: adminUid`.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for Nightcap Nosh prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering to the core design principles. The key strategy for achieving **Authorization Independence** is the aggressive denormalization of authorization context directly into the documents that require it. This eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in security rules, which can break atomic operations and are difficult to debug.

For user-specific data such as `UserProfile`, `Address`, and `UserReward`, a hierarchical path structure (`/users/{userId}/...`) is employed. Here, the `userId` from `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, inherently securing access to a user's own data. Documents in subcollections like `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}` also contain a `userId` field that mirrors the parent's `userId`, reinforcing ownership and ensuring rules can validate access without traversing up the hierarchy.

For collaborative or multi-party entities like `Order` and `ChatSession`, a top-level collection (`/orders/{orderId}` and `/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}`) is used. This approach facilitates **Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs)**. Instead of relying on a parent document to determine access, each `Order` document explicitly includes `userId` (for the customer) and `driverId` (for the assigned driver). Similarly, each `ChatSession` document contains `userId` (for the customer) and `agentId` (for the support agent). This denormalization allows security rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` or `request.auth.uid == resource.data.driverId`/`agentId`, enabling efficient and secure `list` operations without requiring additional `get()` calls to other collections. For `OrderItem` and `ChatMessage` subcollections, this denormalization extends, meaning `userId` and `driverId`/`agentId` are copied from their respective parent `Order` or `ChatSession` into each item/message document. This ensures that even nested documents can be authorized independently.

**Structural Segregation** is applied by using top-level collections for publicly readable entities (`Category`, `Product`, `Pairing`, `Reward`) and a dedicated `/admin_roles/{adminUid}` collection for database-backed access control (DBAC). Admin users' UIDs are stored in `admin_roles`, allowing rules to grant administrative write access by simply checking `get(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`. This clear separation ensures that security postures are homogeneous within collections, simplifying rule logic significantly. The `status` field in `Order`, `ChatSession`, and `Driver` documents provides **Explicit State Modeling**, ensuring predictable data behavior and simpler rule conditions based on documented states.
